discourse.name: Euclid's Elements, Book X
discourse.subject: incommensurables
discourse.description: Incommensurables, binomials, apotomes. 16 definitions, 115 propositions. Depends on Books I, V, VI. Source: David E. Joyce.

metadata.citation: Welz, G. (2026). Euclid's Elements Book X Dependency Graph. Programming Framework.

nodes.label: Book I — Plane geometry
Book V — Proportions
Book VI — Similar figures
Two unequal magnitudes: subtract more than half repeatedly; remainder less than lesser
If less subtracted from greater repeatedly and remainder never measures prior: incommensurable
To find greatest common measure of two commensurable magnitudes
To find greatest common measure of three commensurable magnitudes
Commensurable magnitudes have ratio which a number has to a number
If two magnitudes have number-to-number ratio, they are commensurable
Incommensurable magnitudes do not have number-to-number ratio
If magnitudes lack number-to-number ratio, they are incommensurable
Squares on commensurable lines have square-to-square ratio; converse
To find two lines incommensurable with assigned: one in length, one in square
Four proportional: first commensurable with second iff third with fourth
Magnitudes commensurable with same magnitude are commensurable
If two commensurable, one incommensurable with third, so is the other
Given two lines: find square by which greater square exceeds less; find line whose square equals sum
Two commensurable added: whole commensurable with each
Two incommensurable added: sum incommensurable with each
Applied parallelogram falling short by square: parts commensurable iff square difference commensurable
Applied parallelogram: parts incommensurable iff square difference incommensurable
Rectangle by rational lines commensurable in length is rational
Rational area applied to rational line: breadth rational and commensurable
Rectangle by rational in square only is irrational; side called medial
Square on medial applied to rational: breadth rational, incommensurable in length
Line commensurable with medial is medial
Rectangle by medial lines commensurable in length is medial
Rectangle by medial in square only: rational or medial
Medial area does not exceed medial by rational area
To find medial lines commensurable in square only containing rational rectangle
nodes.short: Find medial in square only, rational rect

nodes.label: To find medial lines commensurable in square only containing medial rectangle
nodes.short: Find medial in square only, medial rect

nodes.label: To find two rational in square only: square diff commensurable with greater
nodes.short: Find rational in square only, commensurable diff

nodes.label: To find two rational in square only: square diff incommensurable with greater
nodes.short: Find rational in square only, incommensurable diff

nodes.label: To find two medial in square only, rational rect: diff commensurable with greater
nodes.short: Find medial, rational rect, commensurable diff

nodes.label: To find two medial in square only, medial rect: diff commensurable with greater
nodes.short: Find medial, medial rect, commensurable diff

nodes.label: To find two incommensurable in square: sum of squares rational, rectangle medial
nodes.short: Find incommensurable: rational sum, medial rect

nodes.label: To find two incommensurable in square: sum medial, rectangle rational
nodes.short: Find incommensurable: medial sum, rational rect

nodes.label: To find two incommensurable in square: sum and rect medial, incommensurable
Two rational in square only added: whole irrational, called binomial
Two medial in square only, rational rect added: first bimedial
Two medial in square only, medial rect added: second bimedial
Two incommensurable in square, rational sum, medial rect added: major
Two incommensurable in square, medial sum, rational rect added: side of rational plus medial
Two incommensurable in square, both medial added: side of sum of two medial
nodes.short: Side of sum of two medial

nodes.label: Binomial divided into terms at one point only
First bimedial divided at one point only
Second bimedial divided at one point only
Major divided at one point only
Side of rational plus medial divided at one point only
Side of sum of two medial divided at one point only
To find the first binomial line
To find the second binomial line
To find the third binomial line
To find the fourth binomial line
To find the fifth binomial line
To find the sixth binomial line
Area by rational and first binomial: side is binomial
Area by rational and second binomial: side is first bimedial
Area by rational and third binomial: side is second bimedial
Area by rational and fourth binomial: side is major
Area by rational and fifth binomial: side is rational plus medial
Area by rational and sixth binomial: side is sum of two medial
Square on binomial applied to rational: breadth first binomial
Square on first bimedial applied to rational: breadth second binomial
Square on second bimedial applied to rational: breadth third binomial
Square on major applied to rational: breadth fourth binomial
Square on rational+medial applied to rational: breadth fifth binomial
Square on sum of two medial applied to rational: breadth sixth binomial
Line commensurable with binomial is binomial, same order
Line commensurable with bimedial is bimedial, same order
Line commensurable with major is major
Line commensurable with rational+medial is rational+medial
Line commensurable with sum of two medial is sum of two medial
Rational and medial added: four irrationals arise
Two medial incommensurable added: two irrationals arise
Rational minus rational in square only: remainder irrational, apotome
Medial minus medial, rational rect: first apotome of medial
Medial minus medial, medial rect: second apotome of medial
Line minus incommensurable: sum rational, rect medial: remainder minor
Line minus incommensurable: sum medial, rect rational: produces rational+medial
Line minus incommensurable: both medial, incommensurable: produces medial+medial
To apotome only one rational can be annexed in square only
To first apotome of medial: unique medial annex, rational rect
To second apotome of medial: unique medial annex, medial rect
To minor: unique annex incommensurable in square
To produces rational+medial: unique annex
To produces medial+medial: unique annex
To find the first apotome
To find the second apotome
To find the third apotome
To find the fourth apotome
To find the fifth apotome
To find the sixth apotome
Area by rational and first apotome: side is apotome
Area by rational and second apotome: side is first apotome of medial
Area by rational and third apotome: side is second apotome of medial
Area by rational and fourth apotome: side is minor
Area by rational and fifth apotome: side produces rational+medial
Area by rational and sixth apotome: side produces medial+medial
Square on apotome of medial applied to rational: breadth first apotome
Square on first apotome of medial: breadth second apotome
Square on second apotome of medial: breadth third apotome
Square on minor applied to rational: breadth fourth apotome
Square on produces rational+medial: breadth fifth apotome
Square on produces medial+medial: breadth sixth apotome
Line commensurable with apotome is apotome, same order
Line commensurable with apotome of medial is apotome of medial
Line commensurable with minor is minor
Line commensurable with produces rational+medial is same
Line commensurable with produces medial+medial is same
From rational area subtract medial: side is apotome or minor
From medial subtract rational: first apotome of medial or rational+medial
From medial subtract medial incommensurable: second apotome or medial+medial
Apotome is not the same as binomial
Square on rational applied to binomial: breadth apotome, same order
Square on rational applied to apotome: breadth binomial, same order
Area by apotome and binomial (commensurable terms): side is rational
From medial arise irrationals infinite in number, none same as preceding
